rationale: The citing paper explicitly targets cross-media advertising effectiveness (Internet formats vs TV) and positions itself against prior limited cross-media evidence, while the cited paper is a core multimedia-communications study on cross-media synergy and media-allocation implications. That topical overlap suggests the citation could be a substantive theory/background anchor rather than a purely incidental mention. Given only DOI-level matched evidence (no local citation sentence), this should be retained for second-stage relation judgment.
